perturbation.original_text: 1.  TERM OF AGREEMENT 1.1 The term of this Agreement shall commence on the date hereof and terminate upon the conclusion of the week following the conclusion of the Dolphins 2000 regular season or post season, if applicable (the "Term" or "Initial Term"). However, this Agreement may be earlier terminated in accordance with the provisions hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.  TERM OF AGREEMENT 1.1 The term of this Agreement shall commence on the date hereof and terminate upon the conclusion of the week following the conclusion of the Dolphins 2000 regular season or post season, if applicable (the "Duration" or "Initial Term"). However, this Agreement may be earlier terminated in accordance with the provisions hereof.
perturbation.explanation: The original text defines "Term" and "Initial Term" as the same thing. This change redefines "Term" as "Duration" while leaving "Initial Term" unchanged. This introduces confusion, as later sections rely on a consistent definition of "Term". The change creates in-text contradiction on section 2.1
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. SPONSORSHIP RIGHTS 2.1 For the Term of this Agreement, the Dolphins will provide Hydron with certain advertising and promotional benefits as are set forth in and in accordance with Exhibit "A" attached hereto and made a part hereof (the "Sponsorship Rights").
perturbation.justification: The change redefines "Term" as "Duration" while "Initial Term" remains unchanged. Section 2.1 explicitly states "For the Term of this Agreement", implying consistency. Now, because "Term" has been changed to "Duration", while the agreement continues to use 'Initial Term', it creates ambiguity and in-text contradiction regarding the duration of the 'Sponsorship Rights', thereby creating in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 3. CONSIDERATION 3.1 In consideration of the Sponsorship Rights granted to Hydron hereunder: (a) Hydron shall pay to the Dolphins an aggregate amount of $96,000 (plus any applicable sales and other taxes) as follows: Hydron shall pay Dolphins the sum of $24,000 (plus any applicable sales and other taxes) on July 1, 1997, July 1, 1998, July 1, 1999 and July 1, 2000.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. CONSIDERATION 3.1 In consideration of the Sponsorship Rights granted to Hydron hereunder: (a) Hydron shall pay to the Dolphins an aggregate amount of $96,000 (plus any applicable sales and other taxes) as follows: Hydron shall pay Dolphins the sum of $24,000 (plus any applicable sales and other taxes) on August 1, 1997, August 1, 1998, August 1, 1999 and August 1, 2000.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states payments are due on July 1st, This change modifies that date to August 1st. This creates a contradiction because the actual due date becomes ambiguous, which causes a contradiction on section 3.2.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.2 In the event that the consideration is not paid by Hydron on or before the applicable payment due date, said failure to pay shall be considered a material breach by Hydron, and the Dolphins may elect to charge Hydron a late fee of 1.5% per month of the payment then due and owing until it is paid in full. The Dolphins agree to provide written notice to Hydron of the failure to receive any payment, and Hydron shall have a five (5) business day period following delivery of written notice in which to cure the payment default before the Dolphins may elect to terminate this Agreement and pursue applicable remedies. It is agreed by Hydron that any such election of remedies does not waive any other remedies for breach of contract available to the Dolphins.
perturbation.justification: The change from "July 1" to "August 1" for the payment due date directly contradicts section 3.2, which refers to the "applicable payment due date." This creates uncertainty about which date is correct, impacting the material breach clause and the ability to charge late fees based on a clear, enforceable deadline. The modification introduces ambiguity where clarity is expected, fitting the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' because it introduces conflicting deadlines.

perturbation.original_text: 7.2 Insurance. The Dolphins shall, at its own expense, maintain in effect throughout the term of this Agreement, comprehensive general liability insurance policies with carriers of recognized standing, with limits of liability of at least One Million Dollars ($1,000,000), governing any and all property damage and person injury (including death) arising out of activities covered by this Agreement. Hydron shall, at its own expense, maintain in effect throughout the term of this Agreement, comprehensive general liability insurance policies with carriers of recognized standing, with limits of liability of at least One Million Dollars ($1,000,000), covering any and all property damage and personal injury (including death) arising out of activities covered by this Agreement and shall obtain and maintain such additional insurance coverage as the Dolphins shall reasonably require with respect to any Sponsored Events or similar activities.
perturbation.changed_text: 7.2 Insurance. The Dolphins shall, at its own expense, maintain in effect throughout the term of this Agreement, comprehensive general liability insurance policies with carriers of recognized standing, with limits of liability of at least One Million Dollars ($1,000,000), governing any and all property damage and person injury (including death) arising out of activities covered by this Agreement. Hydron shall, at its own expense, maintain in effect throughout the term of this Agreement, minimum general liability insurance policies with carriers of questionable standing, with limits of liability of at least Five Hundred Thousand Dollars ($500,000), covering any and all property damage and personal injury (including death) arising out of activities covered by this Agreement and shall obtain and maintain such additional insurance coverage as the Dolphins shall reasonably require with respect to any Sponsored Events or similar activities.
perturbation.explanation: This change affects the insurance requirements for Hydron. The original text requires 'comprehensive general liability insurance policies with carriers of recognized standing' but the modified version specifies 'minimum general liability insurance policies with carriers of questionable standing'. This introduces an internal contradiction as the new insurance standards for Hydron are ambigious, it causes contradiction to section 7.1
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7.1 Each of the parties shall indemnify and hold harmless the other, and their respective partners, shareholders, officers, employees, agents and representatives (collectively, the "Indemnitees") from and against any and all claims, orders, damages, liabilities, costs and expenses, including reasonable attorney's fees, arising out of the other party's negligent actions or omissions with respect to this Agreement, or such party's wilful misconduct or breach of any representation, warranty or agreement in this Agreement applicable to it. Neither party shall have an obligation to indemnify or hold harmless an Indemnitees from any claim arising from or related to the Indemnitees negligence or misconduct. Each party hereto shall promptly notify the other of any claim or litigation to which the indemnity set forth in this paragraph applies. Each of the parties agree to defend all actions to which such indemnity applies and to conduct the defense thereof at its expense and by qualified counsel, which counsel shall be reasonably satisfactory to the Indemnitees. Each of the parties agree that the foregoing indemnities also apply for the benefit of the NFL (and its affiliates), South Florida Stadium Corporation, the owner and operator of Pro Player Stadium and their respective officials, officers, partners, agents and employees, who shall be deemed third party beneficiaries of this Agreement for the purpose of enforcing these indemnity obligations. These indemnity obligations shall survive the termination or expiration of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The modified text changes the insurance requirements for Hydron from 'comprehensive general liability insurance policies with carriers of recognized standing' to 'minimum general liability insurance policies with carriers of questionable standing.' This directly contradicts section 7.1, which requires each party to indemnify and hold harmless the other from liabilities arising out of their negligent actions or omissions. The lower insurance coverage and questionable standing of the carrier directly impacts the ability of Hydron to fulfill its indemnification obligations under 7.1, creating a significant in-text contradiction. If Hydron's insurance is inadequate, the Dolphins may not be fully protected against claims, thus violating the indemnification agreement.
